key_insight: MOSAIC improves continual Parkinson’s gait assessment by adapting each sensor modality separately while preserving shared disease knowledge across changing multimodal data streams.
engineering_relevance: The framework is relevant for real-world clinical sensing pipelines where new devices, missing modalities, and privacy limits make retraining on all historical data impractical.
method: Modality-Specific Warm-Up to stabilize new modality features before distillation
Statistics-decoupled MSBN to separate sensor-specific statistics from a shared semantic backbone
Curriculum-guided repulsive objective to recover plasticity while limiting forgetting
evidence_signal: The abstract reports evaluation on three multimodal Parkinson’s gait datasets, with improved final performance and reduced forgetting.
watch_item: The approach adds architectural and training complexity, so stable tuning across modalities and datasets may be a practical deployment concern.
paper_title: MOSAIC: Modality-Specific Adaptation for Incremental Continual Learning in Parkinson's Disease Gait Assessment
